title: Ecbolium spergulifolium
taxonomy: Ecbolium spergulifolium is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. It belongs to the genus Ecbolium, which is a small group of flowering plants. Ecbolium spergulifolium is native to tropical Africa and is found in various regions, including Cameroon and the Democratic Republic of the Congo. The classification of Ecbolium spergulifolium reflects its close relationship with other plants within the Acanthaceae family. Further research may be needed to confirm its exact taxonomic classification within the plant kingdom.

intro: Ecbolium spergulifolium is a plant species belonging to the family Acanthaceae, commonly known as a flowering plant. Traditionally, it has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues due to its antimalarial, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Ecbolium spergulifolium include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Ecbolium spergulifolium is often prepared as a decoction or infusion to treat various conditions, and it is also used in traditional remedies such as teas and tinctures.
